sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports right knee pain, exacerbated by valgus stressing, and describes using ice and ibuprofen with limited relief. He has a history of atrial fibrillation and is currently taking digoxin. He reports a previous rhinoplasty and denies any prior surgeries other than the nose job.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Examination reveals tenderness over the medial portion of the left knee, specifically over the medial meniscus area. X-rays demonstrate no fractures or dislocations, but reveal some joint fluid. No abnormalities were noted during neurological examination.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient is likely experiencing an acute medial meniscus sprain or strain, based on the examination findings and reported symptoms.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I recommend a knee brace and temporary crutches for the next couple of days. Prescribe 800 mg of ibuprofen as needed. Schedule a follow-up appointment in approximately one week to assess improvement and consider an MRI if symptoms persist.
